The pillar is up to close range wide and rises up to a short distance high, with a flat top that creatures can stand on. You choose its exact height when it rises. Any creature standing on the pillar is carried up with it. An unwilling creature can roll Agility + Athletics vs. your Resist to leap off before it rises, landing prone in an adjacent space.
If the pillar meets a ceiling or other solid obstacle, it stops. A creature caught between the pillar and the obstacle takes 8 damage (ignore AV) and is squeezed into the gap, which counts as a constricted area.
The pillar is ordinary stone. It remains after the spell, can be climbed with a Strength + Athletics roll, and can only be removed by mundane means or magic that affects stone. When you cast this spell again, all pillars from your previous casting crumble into harmless rubble as the new one rises.
heightened:
(Rank 4) The pillar can rise up to a medium distance high instead, and you may raise up to two pillars with one casting, each up to close range wide and placed anywhere in range. A creature caught between a pillar and an obstacle takes 10 damage (ignore AV) instead.
(Rank 5) You may raise up to three pillars with one casting, each up to close range wide, each rising up to a medium distance high, and placed anywhere in range. A creature caught between a pillar and an obstacle takes 12 damage (ignore AV) instead.

name: Control Weather
effect:
You commune with sky and season, and the weather bends by slow degrees to the shape of your will. Through a ritual of minutes you begin to turn the prevailing weather across the surrounding region, then hold and deepen it for as long as you concentrate (up to a day of dedicated tending). You may shift the weather one stage at a time toward a chosen condition, never conjuring what the season and land could not plausibly produce. Choose the target condition:
- Clear. Skies open and the air stills. Any weather-based obscurement, difficult terrain, or wind effect across the region ends, and rolls to travel or to see over long distances through the region gain +1 boon.
- Rain. A steady downpour soaks the region. The land beyond short range is lightly obscured, unsheltered open flames are extinguished and mundane fires struggle to catch, and empty vessels and cisterns fill with clean water.
- Storm. A tempest of driving rain and wind builds. The region is difficult terrain (including for flight) and ranged attacks through it suffer +1 bane. A creature that travels through the storm without shelter gains +1 Fatigue for the day.
- Fog. A thick mist settles. The land beyond short range is heavily obscured, and creatures gain +1 boon on Stealth rolls to hide within it.
- Heat or Cold. The air turns oppressively hot or bitterly cold. A creature that goes a day without proper protection (shade and water against the heat, warmth against the cold) gains +1 Fatigue.
Ending your concentration lets the natural weather reassert itself over the following hours.

name: Tree Stride
effect:
You step into the living wood and the tree takes you in like an old friend, passing you along the green road to emerge far away. You enter a living tree at least your own size that you touch. While the spell lasts, up to a medium duration while you concentrate, you may step into such a tree and emerge from another living tree of similar size within extreme range as your movement, once per turn.
When you enter a tree, your awareness spreads through the green road and you sense every living tree of similar size within range, even those out of sight. To travel, name where you wish to emerge, such as a tree you can see, a known landmark, or a direction and rough distance, and you step out of the nearest suitable tree to that point. If no tree large enough stands within range of your chosen destination, you sense this before you commit and simply do not step, losing nothing. The GM tells you what trees are within reach whenever the surroundings make it uncertain, such as sparse woodland, a lone orchard, or open country where few or none may qualify.
When the spell ends or your concentration breaks, you are expelled from the nearest tree unharmed.
heightened: (Rank 5) You may carry up to five willing creatures who touch you through the green road with each stride, and the range extends across any forested expanse in the same region.

name: Gust
effect:
You summon a vortex of swirling air, clashing against your target and pulling them off their feet.
Weak. Deal +2 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV).
Strong. Deal +4 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) and push the target close.
Critical. Deal +6 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) and push the target a short distance.
You can also cast this spell to create a continuous stream of air from your palm that has enough pressure to move small, loose objects in range.

name: Static Shock
effect:
You generate a small electric discharge from your fingertip against a target.
Weak. Deal +2 lightning damage.
Strong. Deal +4 lightning damage to the target and you can deal +2 lightning damage to one other creature in melee range of the target.
Critical. Deal +6 lightning damage to the target and you can deal +4 lightning damage to one other creature in melee range of the target.
You can also cast this spell to ignite flammable materials in range, such as tinder or straw.

name: Wind Slash
effect:
Hurl a blade of compressed air against your target.
Weak. Deal +0 damage or +4 damage against a target in light or no armor.
Strong. Deal +2 damage or +6 damage against a target in light or no armor.
Critical. Deal +4 damage or +8 damage against a target in light or no armor.
You can also use this spell to chip off or cut parts of softer materials such as wood or papyrus in range.

name: Tremor
effect:
You stamp your heel and the ground answers, a shudder running through the earth beneath one creature.
On a success, the target is briefly staggered.
You can also cast this spell to shake dust, loose stones, or small objects free from a surface in range.

name: Sand Gust
effect:
A whip of wind gathers grit and sand and flings it stinging into the target's face.
On a success, grit fills the target's eyes and they briefly suffer +1 bane on all rolls requiring sight, including attack rolls.
You can also cast this spell to scour sand, dust, or grime from a surface or object in range.

name: Splash
effect:
You call a sudden rush of water out of the empty air and send it crashing down.
When targeting a creature, roll vs. their Dodge. Otherwise roll vs. TN 8.
On a success, the target is drenched. Any burning condition on them ends, ordinary open flames they carry or that burn in their melee range are doused, and a drenched creature is briefly slowed by the weight of the water.
Cast on an unattended fire, the water douses flames up to the size of a campfire.

name: Weather Prediction
effect:
You wet a finger, read the wind, and listen to what the sky intends.
On a success, you learn the natural weather coming to the region you stand in over the next day. The GM tells you its kind (such as clear, rain, storm, heat, cold, or sandstorm), roughly when it arrives, and how severe it will be. This spell reads only the natural course of the sky. It gives no insight into weather moved by magic or by a will beyond mortals.

name: Bursting Crackle
effect:
You gather an electric charge at a single point within range and release it with a powerful blast, creating loud thunder. Target all creatures in melee range of the target location.
Weak. Deal +2 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) against each target.
Strong. Deal +4 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) against each target and they are briefly dazed and deafened.
Critical. Deal +6 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) against each target and they are briefly dazed and deafened.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Choose one:
- Deal +4/+8/+12 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) to each target instead.
- Target all creatures in close range of the target location instead.
(Rank 3) Choose one:
- Deal +6/+12/+18 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) to each target instead.
- Target all creatures in short range of the target location instead.

name: Curse of Tempest
effect:
You curse a creature with the magical power of tempest, turning nature itself against them.
On a success, the creature suffers the following effects:
- While moving in natural environments, it is always considered difficult terrain for them.
- They suffer +1 bane on Athletics and Survival rolls.
This curse lasts for a long duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The target suffers a weakness to blast, fire, or lightning damage (choose one) for the spell‘s duration.
(Rank 3) The target suffers a weakness to blast, fire, and lightning damage for the spell‘s duration.

name: Earthen Tremor
effect:
You send shockwaves through the earth, causing the ground to tremble violently. Target all creatures in the spell’s area.
Weak. Deal +2 damage to each target.
Strong. Deal +4 damage to each target and they are briefly staggered.
Critical. Deal +6 damage to each target and they are briefly staggered.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Choose one:
- Deal +3/+6/+9 damage to each target instead.
- Increase the spell‘s range to medium.
(Rank 3) Choose one:
- Deal +4/+8/+12 damage to each target instead.
- Increase the spell‘s range to long.

name: Electrified Weapon
effect:
Imbue a weapon with the power of storms. If the target is dual-wielding, you can instead target both their held weapons.
On a success, the weapon deals +2 lightning damage. On a strong or critical hit, the attack‘s target is briefly staggered. Each creature can suffer the condition from this spell only once per scene. If you target your own weapon, you can make a weapon attack with it as part of casting this spell.
This spell lasts for a medium duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The weapon deals +4 lightning damage instead.
(Rank 3) The weapon deals +6 lightning damage instead.

name: Lightning Javelin
effect:
You conjure pure lightning in the form of a spear and hurl it against a creature.
Weak. Deal +4 lightning damage.
Strong. Deal +8 lightning damage and the target is briefly staggered.
Critical. Deal +12 lightning damage and the target is briefly staggered.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Deal +6/+12/+18 lightning damage instead.
(Rank 3) Deal +8/+16/+24 lightning damage instead.

name: Storm Coat
effect:
You weave a protective coat of elemental energies around a creature.
On a success, the creature gains the following effects:
- You gain resistance against blast, frost, and lightning damage.
- Whenever you are hit by a melee attack, the attacker takes +0 lightning damage (ignore AV).
heightened:
(Rank 2) The attacker takes +2 lightning damage (ignore AV) instead.
(Rank 3) The attacker takes +4 lightning damage (ignore AV) instead.

name: Storm Shield
effect:
You quickly conjure a personal barrier of swirling air. Cast this spell when you are attacked vs. your Parry or Dodge.
On a success, you can defend with your Resist instead of Parry or Dodge until your next turn. When a melee attack against you fails, the attacker takes +0 blast damage (ignore AV). If a ranged attack against you fails, you can divert the attack towards another creature in close range. If the ranged attack blunders, you can even divert it back to the attacker. When diverting an attack to another creature, it automatically hits with a weak success.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Failed melee attacks inflict +2 blast damage (ignore AV) instead. Diverted ranged attacks deal +2 damage.
(Rank 3) Failed melee attacks inflict +4 blast damage (ignore AV) instead. Diverted ranged attacks deal +4 damage.

name: Volcanic Bolt
effect:
You magically dissolve some earth or rock in your surroundings into lava. You can hurl the lava as a searing projectile against a creature.
Weak. Deal +4 fire damage.
Strong. Deal +8 fire damage and they suffer burning (2) for a short duration.
Critical. Deal +12 fire damage and they suffer burning (4) for a short duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Deal +6/+12/+18 fire damage instead and inflict burning (4/6) on a strong or critical success.
(Rank 3) Deal +8/+16/+24 fire damage instead and inflict burning (6/8) on a strong or critical success.

name: Wind Hose
effect:
You create a powerful swirl of air around the target location, pulling smaller, loose objects into it’s vortex.
Weak. Deal +3 damage.
Strong. Deal +6 damage and if the target is medium or smaller in size, they are also grappled by the wind hose.
Critical. Deal +9 damage and if the target is medium or smaller in size, they are also grappled and restrained by the wind hose.
When you successfully cast this spell against a target and it is grappled by the wind hose, you gain +1 boon when casting the spell again against the same target on your next turn.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Choose one:
- Deal +5/+10/+15 damage instead.
- You can also target large creatures for grappling.
(Rank 3) Choose one:
- Deal +7/+14/+21 damage instead.
- You can also target huge creatures for grappling.

name: Sinking Sands
effect:
At your gesture the ground loosens and churns, sand turning to a hungry, sucking mire.
On a success, the ground in close range of the target location turns to quicksand for a short duration while you concentrate. When your concentration ends, the ground settles and any creature grappled by the sands is freed. The area is difficult terrain. A creature that enters the area or starts their turn there must succeed on a Strength + Athletics roll vs. your Resist or they are grappled by the sands. A creature grappled this way can free themselves with a successful Strength + Athletics roll vs. your Resist as an Action.
Creatures standing on solid ground can pull a grappled creature free with a successful Strength + Athletics roll vs. your Resist as an Action.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The quicksand covers short range of the target location instead.
(Rank 3) The quicksand covers short range of the target location instead. A creature starting their turn grappled by the sands must succeed on the same roll or they are also restrained.
(Rank 4) The quicksand covers medium range of the target location instead. A creature starting their turn grappled by the sands must succeed on the same roll or they are also restrained.
(Rank 5) The quicksand covers medium range of the target location instead. A creature starting their turn grappled by the sands must succeed on the same roll or they are also restrained. A creature starting their turn restrained must succeed on the same roll or they are pulled under and suffocating until freed.

name: Water Jet
effect:
You draw the water out of air and earth into a single hard stream and let it slam home.
Weak. Deal +3 damage (ignore 1/2 AV).
Strong. Deal +6 damage (ignore 1/2 AV) and push the target close.
Critical. Deal +9 damage (ignore 1/2 AV), push the target a short distance, and they fall prone.
On any success, the target is drenched and any burning condition on them ends.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Deal +5/+10/+15 damage (ignore 1/2 AV) instead.
(Rank 3) Deal +7/+14/+21 damage (ignore 1/2 AV) instead.
(Rank 4) Deal +9/+18/+27 damage (ignore 1/2 AV) instead.
(Rank 5) Deal +11/+22/+33 damage (ignore 1/2 AV) instead.

name: Conjure Elemental
effect:
You conjure a being of pure elemental power. Choose either air, earth, fire, or water.
On a success, an elemental creature of the chosen element appears at any point within range.
Treat the elemental as your companion (see Mounts & Companions for more). It is friendly towards you and your allies while you maintain concentration for this spell.
The elemental has the statistics of a tier 2 companion. In addition, it gains a companion trait, according to which element you have chosen.
You can dismiss the elemental as a Quick Action during your turn. Otherwise it remains until reduced to 0 HP or for a short duration.
If you lose concentration on this spell, the elemental becomes hostile to you and your allies. Roll initiative for it and treat it as an enemy. You can’t dismiss the elemental anymore and it remains until reduced to 0 HP or after the spell’s duration.
heightened: (Rank 3) You conjure an elemental with the statistics of a tier 3 companion instead.

name: Lightning Bolt
effect:
You summon a powerful bolt of lightning that streaks forth in a straight line from you to a target point up to a short distance away, electrifying every creature it passes.
Weak. Deal +3 lightning damage to each target.
Strong. Deal +6 lightning damage to each target and they are briefly staggered.
Critical. Deal +9 lightning damage to each target and they are briefly staggered.
heightened:
(Rank 3) Choose one:
- Deal +4/+8/+12 lightning damage to each target instead.
- Increase the spell‘s range to long.

name: Lightning Step
effect:
In a sudden flash of lightning you move at the speed of sound to another place. Cast this spell on your turn.
Choose any point within range you can see, where you would be able to move to by walking, climbing, swimming, or jumping.
On a success, you instantly move to the chosen location unprovoked (this isn’t teleportation). When you arrive, you produce a loud booming sound, dealing +2 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) to all creatures in close range of you. Your Movement for the rest of the turn becomes 0.
heightened: (Rank 3) Increase the spell’s range to long and deal +4 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) instead.

name: Magma Burst
effect:
You channel the earth’s wrath, making the target location’s ground burst with lava. Target all creatures in melee range of it.
Weak. Deal +4 fire damage to each target.
Strong. Deal +8 fire damage to each target and they suffer burning (2) for a short duration.
Critical. Deal +12 fire damage to each target and they suffer burning (4) for a short duration.
The target location remains covered with cracks filled with lava for a short duration, after which it cools down and remains as solid rock.
Each creature ending their turn in melee range of it automatically suffer the effects of a weak hit.
If any creature has direct contact with the lava, they instead automatically suffer the effects of a critical hit.
heightened:
(Rank 3) Choose one:
- Deal +6/+12/+18 fire damage to each target and inflict burning (4/6) on a strong or critical success instead.
- Target all creatures in close range of the target location instead.

name: Pyroclasm
effect:
You slam the earth before you and it erupts with force, spitting flaming hot lava into the air.
Weak. Deal +3 fire damage to each target.
Strong. Deal +6 fire damage to each target and they fall prone.
Critical. Deal +9 fire damage to each target, they fall prone, and their Movement briefly becomes 0.
The area affected by this spell remains as broken ground and is considered difficult terrain.
heightened:
(Rank 3) Choose one:
- Deal +4/+8/+12 fire damage to each target instead.
- Increase the spell‘s range to medium.

name: Storm Cloud
effect:
You summon a dark cloud brewing with electricity. On a success, the cloud extends in close range of the target location and hovers up to a medium distance above it. Target up to three creatures below the cloud use your roll’s result vs. their Dodge.
Weak. Deal +3 lightning damage against each target.
Strong. Deal +6 lightning damage against each target.
Critical. Deal +9 lightning damage against each target.
On each of your following turns, you can use your Action to target another one creature with this spell and repeat the roll vs. Dodge. Before or after the roll, you can choose to move the cloud a short distance within range.
The cloud lasts for a short duration.
heightened:
(Rank 3) Choose one:
- Deal +4/+8/+12 lightning damage to each target instead.
- Target up to five creatures below the cloud instead.

name: Thunder Clap
effect:
You intensely clap your hands, summoning a thunderous shockwave that ripples outward from you. Target all creatures in range.
Weak. Deal +2 blast (ignore 1/2 AV) damage to each target.
Strong. Deal +4 blast (ignore 1/2 AV) damage to each target and they are knocked prone and briefly deafened.
Critical. Deal +6 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) to each target and they are pushed close, knocked prone, and briefly deafened.
heightened:
(Rank 3) Choose one:
- Deal +3/+6/+9 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) to each target instead.
- Increase the spell‘s range to medium.

name: Torrent
effect:
You create a high pressure stream of water, pulling anything in it’s way along with it.
Weak. Deal +3 damage to each target and they are pushed a close distance.
Strong. Deal +6 damage to each target and they are pushed a short distance.
Critical. Deal +9 damage to each target and they are pushed a short distance and fall prone.
heightened:
(Rank 3) Choose one:
- Deal +4/+8/+12 damage to each target instead.
- Increase the spell‘s range to long.

name: Wind Ward
effect:
A swirling storm forms around you and follows your movements. On a success, you create a strong wind centered around you and extending in close range. While the wind remains, it has the following effects:
- You and every other creature in the wind’s area is deafened.
- Small sources of flames (e.g. torches) are extinguished in the wind’s area.
- The wind’s area is considered difficult terrain for every creature besides you.
- Any ranged attacks moving in, out, or through the wind’s are suffer +1 bane.
- While in the wind’s area, every other creature takes +2 damage at the start of each of their turns.
The wind remains for a medium duration.
heightened: (Rank 3) The wind extends in short range of you and other creatures in the wind’s area takes +4 damage at the start of each of their turns instead.

name: Thunderfall
effect:
The air above the target gathers into a single held breath of the storm, then falls all at once like a hammer of thunder.
Weak. Deal +2 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV).
Strong. Deal +4 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV).
Critical. Deal +6 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV).
If the target is prone or staggered, deal +6/+12/+18 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) instead and they are briefly deafened, as the full weight of the sky lands on a body that cannot brace.
heightened:
(Rank 3) Deal +3/+6/+9 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV), or +8/+16/+24 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) against a prone or staggered target instead.
(Rank 4) Deal +4/+8/+12 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV), or +10/+20/+30 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) against a prone or staggered target instead.
(Rank 5) Deal +5/+10/+15 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV), or +12/+24/+36 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) against a prone or staggered target instead.

name: Chain Lightning
effect:
You loose a bolt that strikes like a whip and then goes hunting, leaping from body to body along the paths of least resistance.
Weak. Deal +8 lightning damage to the target.
Strong. Deal +16 lightning damage to the target.
Critical. Deal +24 lightning damage to the target.
Also on a success, the lightning arcs from the target to up to three additional creatures, each within close range of the previously struck creature, using your roll's result vs. their Dodge. On a hit, each of them takes half as much damage as the first target.
heightened:
(Rank 4) Deal +10/+20/+30 lightning damage to the first target instead.
(Rank 5) Deal +12/+24/+36 lightning damage to the first target instead.

name: Cone of Cold
effect:
You exhale the cold between the stars, and a fan of white silence rolls out from your hands, flash-freezing everything it touches.
Target all creatures in the cone.
Weak. Deal +4 frost damage to each target.
Strong. Deal +8 frost damage to each target.
Critical. Deal +12 frost damage to each target.
Also on a success, each target is briefly slowed. A target that was already slowed is briefly restrained instead, frozen in a shell of ice.
heightened: (Rank 5) Deal +5/+10/+15 frost damage to each target instead.

name: Cyclone
effect:
You create a massive cyclone that engulfs a wide space. On a success, you summon a violent vortex of air engulfing everything within short distance of the target area and a long distance into the air.
Target each creature in the cyclone’s range and use your roll’s result vs. their Dodge.
On a hit, deal +8 damage (or +12 against flying creatures) to each target and they are pushed a short distance into the center of the cyclone. If they are already within the center area of the cyclone, they fall prone instead.
The cyclone’s area is considered difficult terrain.
On each of your following turns, you can use your Action to repeat the roll vs. Dodge against all creatures within the cyclone. Before or after repeating the roll, you can choose to move the cyclone a short distance within range.
The cyclone lasts for a short duration.

name: Lightning Volley
effect:
You form three hovering rays of lightning above you. You can shoot each ray at the same or different targets and roll one attack for each ray.
Weak. The beam deals +4 lightning damage to it’s target and +2 lightning damage against any other creature in their melee range.
Strong. The beam deals +8 lightning damage and +4 lightning damage against any other creature in their melee range.
Critical. The beam deals +12 lightning damage and +6 lightning damage against any other creature in their melee range.

name: Sandstorm
effect:
A wall of stinging grit rises at your call and grinds across the battlefield in a howling ochre haze. On a success, you raise a sandstorm in short range of the target location for up to a medium duration while you concentrate. The area is heavily obscured and difficult terrain. A creature that ends their turn inside takes 8 damage (ignore AV) from the scouring sand.
On each of your following turns, you can use your Quick Action to move the sandstorm a short distance within range.

name: Shattering Orb
effect:
You summon a tiny translucent orb containing a thunder storm within what shatters violently. Target all creatures in short range of the target location within range.
Weak. Deal +3 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) to each target.
Strong. Deal +6 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) to each target and they are knocked prone and briefly deafened.
Critical. Deal +9 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) to each target and they are pushed close, knocked prone, and briefly deafened.

name: Wind Wall
effect:
A intense updraft creates a wall made of wind. Choose any shape for the wall in a continuous path along the ground with up to a medium distance from start to end within range. The wall also extends a short distance into the air.
On a success, the wall appears. Any huge or smaller creature in melee range of the wall takes +8 damage (or +12 against flying creatures) and is pushed a close distance away from it. Anytime a creature comes into melee range with the wall again, this is repeated.
While the wall remains, flying creatures or creatures with gaseous form can’t pass through it. Any ranged attacks (excluding those with large projectiles, such as siege engines or a giant’s boulder) through the wall are diverted upward and automatically miss.
Any non-flying creature can attempt to move through the wind wall. When doing so, the creature can roll a very hard Strength + Athletics test. On a success, they may move through the wall by spending 1 Movement. On a failure, they take +8 damage, are pushed a close distance away from it, and their Movement becomes 0 for the rest of their turn.
The wall remains for a short duration.

name: Avatar of Storms
effect:
You become one with the storm, transforming into a living tempest of elemental energy.
On a success, you gain the following effects:
- You are able to fly and gain +1 Movement per turn.
- You gain 20 temporary HP.
- You are immune to lightning damage and gain resistance against frost and physical damage.
- Whenever you are hit by a melee attack, the attacker takes +4 lightning damage (ignore AV).
- Whenever you deal blast or lightning damage, add your Mysticism to the damage.
This form lasts for a short duration. You can immediately recast this spell as an Action at the end of the duration to remain in this form for a longer time.

name: Control Water
effect:
You control a large amount of water and shape it to your will. Choose an area of water covering a long distance in all directions from the target location within range. In addition, you can choose one of the following options to manipulate the water in your affected area.
Flood. You rise the standing water level in your spell’s area by up to a close distance up (which then counts as a large body of water on your next turn). The water levels stay elevated for a medium duration or until you choose another option for this spell.
If using this option on a large body of water, you instead cause the water in your spell’s area to cause a massive wave that travels from one end of the area to the other and then crashes violently. Any huge or smaller creature or vehicle in the spell’s area is forced to move with the wave and takes +12 damage (ignore 1/2 AV). Aquatic creatures take no damage but are still forced to move with the wave. The flood repeats during each of your turns for a medium duration or until you choose another option for this spell.
Split. Choose any line within the waters area from end to end where you part the water and create a trench with a width covering a close distance. The sides of the trench for a wall of water. The trench remains for a medium duration or until you choose another option for this spell. When the trench closes, the waters snap back violently. Any creature within the closing trench takes +16 damage (ignore 1/2 AV), is briefly staggered, and remains underwater where the trench used to be.
Redirect. You force the water in the spell’s area to flow in any direction of your choosing. Any huge or smaller creature or vehicle in the spell’s area is forced to move with the water’s flow until reaching the end of the spell’s area. The redirected flow remains for a medium duration or until you choose another option for this spell.
Whirlpool. You set the water spinning into a churning vortex centered on a point in the spell’s area, filling a close area around it. At the start of each of their turns, any huge or smaller creature or vehicle in the whirlpool is pulled a short distance toward the center. A creature that starts its turn in the center must roll Strength + Athletics vs. your Resist or be dragged beneath the surface and begin suffocating until they escape the whirlpool. Swimming within the whirlpool counts as difficult terrain. The whirlpool remains for a medium duration or until you choose another option for this spell.
On each of your following turns, you can use your Action to repeat the roll for the same or a different option. If you fail the roll, the spell ends.
heightened: (Rank 5) The affected area covers an extreme distance in all directions from the target location. The Flood option’s wave deals +16 damage (ignore 1/2 AV), and the Split option’s closing trench deals +20 damage (ignore 1/2 AV), instead.

name: Control Winds
effect:
You spread your arms and the sky obeys, winds bending to your will across the whole reach of the land. You command the winds over a large area within range for up to a long duration while you concentrate. Choose one mode, and you may change modes on your turn as a Quick Action:
- Gale. Strong horizontal winds blow in one direction you choose. Ranged attacks through the area suffer +1 bane, airborne gases and fogs disperse, and at the start of each of their turns, unsecured objects and creatures are pushed in the wind's direction: those of Small Size or lighter a short distance, those of Medium Size a close distance. Larger creatures stand firm. Moving against the wind's direction is difficult terrain.
- Downdraft. Winds press downward. Ranged attacks through the area suffer +1 bane. At the start of each of their turns, flying creatures in the area are forced a short distance toward the ground and no creature can gain height while in the area. A flying creature driven all the way to the ground this way lands and is knocked prone.
- Updraft. Winds rush upward. Falls within the area are slowed to a drift (no falling damage). At the start of each of their turns, unsecured objects and willing creatures of Medium Size or lighter can be lifted a short distance upward (a willing creature is one that chooses to be raised).
While you concentrate, the wind's area moves with you, so you can stand aboard a moving vehicle or ship and drive it before a steady gale.

name: Earthquake
effect:
The ground groans and bucks beneath you, stone splitting as the earth throws off everything standing upon it. On a success, you shake the earth in medium range of the target location for up to a short duration while you concentrate. The ground in the area becomes difficult terrain of broken rubble. When the quake begins and at the start of each of your turns while it lasts, roll Spirit + Mysticism vs. Parry against all creatures on the ground in the area. On a success, that creature is knocked prone and takes 8 damage (ignore AV) from falling debris.
Fissures. When the quake begins, up to three fissures tear open at locations of your choice within the area, but never beneath a structure. Each is a short distance wide and up to a medium distance deep. A creature standing where a fissure opens must roll Agility + Athletics vs. your Resist. On a failure, they drop in and take falling damage for the fissure's depth. On a success, they scramble to the nearest edge and stay on solid ground. Climbing out of a fissure takes an Action and a Strength + Athletics roll, and its rubble walls are difficult terrain.
Structures. Freestanding structures and unattended stonework in the area take heavy damage when the quake begins and at the start of each of your turns, and unsound ones collapse. A creature beneath or beside a collapsing structure takes falling damage for the structure's height and is knocked prone.

name: Lightning Storm
effect:
Black thunderheads boil across the whole sky, and at your command the heavens answer in a barrage of searing white spears. On a success, you raise a raging storm within range for up to a short duration while you concentrate. When you cast it and at the start of each of your turns while it lasts, six bolts of lightning crash down at points of your choice within range, no two in the same area. Each bolt strikes every creature in close range of its point. Compare your casting result to the Dodge of each struck creature.
Weak. Deal +6 lightning damage to each struck creature.
Strong. Deal +12 lightning damage to each struck creature and they are briefly dazed.
Critical. Deal +18 lightning damage to each struck creature and they are dazed for a short duration.

name: Aura of Sanctuary
effect:
You shield a creature in range with a magical aura discouraging other creatures from inflicting harm on them. Cast this spell as a Quick Action on your turn.
On a success, the target is shielded by the aura for a short duration. While shielded by the aura, whenever another creature attempts to attack them, the attacker has to roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist. On a failure, they are unable to attack the shielded creature and must choose a new target.
This spell ends early, if the shielded creature attacks any other creature.

name: Calming Influence
effect:
You calm the mind of one creature in range. You suffer +1 bane on this spell, if the target is actively engaged in combat or another dangerous situation.
On a success, the target is shortly charmed by you and will act with empathy and friendliness towards you.

name: Tranquil Mind
effect:
You bring tranquility to the target‘s mind, either to renew their spirits, or to forcefully pacify them. When targeting an ally, roll vs. moderate difficulty (8). When targeting an enemy, roll vs. Resist.
Weak. Restore +2 HP to an ally or deal +0 psychic damage (ignore AV) against an enemy.
Strong. Restore +4 HP to an ally or deal +2 psychic damage (ignore AV) against an enemy and they are briefly dazed.
Critical. Restore +6 HP to an ally or deal +4 psychic damage (ignore AV) against an enemy and they are dazed for a short duration. The enemy can roll Spirit + Fortitude at the end of their turns to end the effect early.

name: Shared Burden
effect:
You lay both hands on a companion and speak the old words of the shared road, drawing their suffering into your own body as a flash of pain.
Choose one condition the touched ally currently suffers among dazed, frightened, slowed, staggered, or poisoned.
On a success, the condition ends on them and you take damage (ignore AV) based on its remaining duration: 2 if it would end briefly, 4 for a short duration, or 6 for a medium or longer duration. This damage cannot be redirected, prevented, or reduced.
This spell cannot end a condition sustained by a persistent source, such as a curse, an illness, or an ongoing effect that reapplies it.

name: Wayfinder's Mark
effect:
You trace a small blessed sign onto a stone, doorpost, tree, or waypost, and it settles into the grain like a promise of return. The mark must be placed on an unmoving structure or feature of the land, never on a creature, a vehicle, or anything else that moves. If the marked object is moved from its place, the mark breaks.
On a success, the mark lasts for one moon. While you are within the same region, you feel a faint pull toward each of your marks. It tells you the rough direction and the distance as a range band, such as a day's march, a thousand paces, or medium range. The pull sharpens as you draw near. Creatures you name aloud as you cast gain +1 boon on rolls to navigate toward the marked place.
You can maintain up to three marks. Placing a fourth fades your oldest mark.

name: Oathmark
effect:
Cast this spell when a creature freely swears an oath aloud in your presence.
On a success, a pale blessed sign settles onto the back of the swearer's hand, plain for all to see. It lasts for one moon or until the oath is fulfilled.
If the marked creature breaks the spoken wording of their oath, you know it the moment it happens while you are within the same region, the mark turns ashen for all to see, and the breaker feels that you know. The mark follows only the oath's literal wording, nothing of its intent.

name: Absorb Harm
effect:
You breathe in the violence meant for another and let it break on your own calm. Cast this spell when you or an ally in close range of you is attacked vs. Parry or Dodge.
On a success, the target of the attack gains +2 to their Parry or Dodge against the triggering attack. If you cast it for an ally and the attack still hits them, half of the damage is dealt to you instead of them.

name: Blessing of Peace
effect:
You bless a creature with the magical power of peace, calming their mind and making others like them more.
On a success, the creature gains the following effects:
- You gain +1 Resist.
- You gain +1 boon on Influence and Insight rolls.
This spell lasts for a long duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The target gains resistance to blast and psychic damage for the spell‘s duration.
(Rank 3) The target gains resistance to blast, physical, and psychic damage for the spell‘s duration.

name: Harmonic Link
effect:
You build a empathetic link with one of your allies, sharing your surface thoughts and senses.
On a success, you establish the link between you and your ally. While the link holds, you gain the following effects:
- If either one of you takes damage, both of you take half of the damage instead.
- You can share simple thoughts and feelings with each other without speaking, and each of you always knows the other's direction and state of health.
- Each of you can use the Help action for the other as a Quick Action on their own turn, without needing to be in melee range of each other.
The link lasts for a medium duration or until you are further away from each other than the range of this spell.
heightened:
(Rank 2) While the link holds you and your ally also gain the following effect(s):
- You gain +1 on all Defenses.
(Rank 3) While the link holds you and your ally also gain the following effect(s):
- You gain +1 on all Defenses.
- Attacks against a target both of you can perceive gain +1 boon.
- Attacks from creatures both of you can perceive suffer +1 bane against you.

name: Swift Journey
effect:
You anoint the sandals and staves of the company at first light, and the road seems to rise to meet their steps.
On a success, you bless yourself and up to six willing travelers for one day. Blessed travelers gain +1 boon on rolls made to travel.
You can only cast this spell once per day.
heightened: (Rank 2) Blessed travelers gain +1 boon on rolls made to travel, and the group gains +1 progress on that day of travel instead.

name: Peace Bond
effect:
You speak a word of law over the target's weapons, and their arms grow heavy with the weight of it.
On a success, the bond lies on the target for a short duration. Their attacks deal -2 damage (min. 0), and each time they make an attack, they take 2 psychic damage (ignore AV) as the bond stings them for raising a hand. They can roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the effect early.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Their attacks deal -4 damage (min. 0) and the sting deals 4 psychic damage (ignore AV) instead.
(Rank 3) Their attacks deal -6 damage (min. 0) and the sting deals 6 psychic damage (ignore AV) instead.

name: Pacifying Weapon
effect:
Imbue a weapon with the power of tranquility. If the target is dual-wielding, you can instead target both their held weapons.
On a success, the weapon‘s bearer gains +1 Parry (doesn‘t stack from dual-wielding weapons enchanted by this spell). On a strong or critical hit, the attack‘s target is briefly dazed. Each creature can suffer the condition from this spell only once per scene. If you target your own weapon, you can make a weapon attack with it as part of casting this spell.
This spell lasts for a medium duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The weapon‘s bearer gains +1 on all Defenses instead (doesn‘t stack from dual-wielding weapons enchanted by this spell).
(Rank 3) The weapon‘s bearer gains +2 on all Defenses instead (doesn‘t stack from dual-wielding weapons enchanted by this spell).

name: Share Harm
effect: Cast this spell after you have taken damage from an attack. On a success, the attacker takes the same amount of damage you took from them (ignore AV).
heightened:
(Rank 2) Add your Mysticism to the damage the attacker takes.
(Rank 3) Add 2 x your Mysticism to the damage the attacker takes.

name: Renewed Resolve
effect:
You speak a word of calm to a defended companion, and the sanctuary's stillness knits their wounds and clears their mind.
Target one ally who is currently affected by one of your *Peace* spells.
Weak. The target regains 3 HP.
Strong. The target regains 6 HP.
Critical. The target regains 9 HP.
On any success, the target also gains +1 boon on the next roll they make.
heightened:
(Rank 3) The target regains 4/8/12 HP instead.
(Rank 4) The target regains 5/10/15 HP instead.
(Rank 5) The target regains 6/12/18 HP instead.

name: Shield of the Meek
effect:
Cast this spell when an enemy damages an ally who is currently affected by one of your *Peace* spells. The ward flares with a sound like a struck bell.
Weak. Deal +3 psychic damage (ignore AV) to the attacker.
Strong. Deal +6 psychic damage (ignore AV) to the attacker.
Critical. Deal +9 psychic damage (ignore AV) to the attacker.
On any success, the attacker also suffers +1 bane on their next attack roll against that ally.
heightened:
(Rank 3) Deal +4/+8/+12 psychic damage (ignore AV) instead.
(Rank 4) Deal +5/+10/+15 psychic damage (ignore AV) instead.
(Rank 5) Deal +6/+12/+18 psychic damage (ignore AV) instead.

name: Herald's Aegis
effect:
You anoint the brow of an envoy, and the old law of the herald settles over them like a mantle all can feel.
Target yourself or one willing creature.
On a success, the target stands under the herald's law for a medium duration. While this spell lasts, any creature near the target instinctively recognizes them as an envoy under sacred law, attack rolls against them suffer +1 bane, and any creature that damages them is briefly dazed by the weight of their transgression. Each creature can suffer the condition from this spell only once per scene.
The blessing ends early if the target makes an attack or casts a spell that deals damage.
heightened: (Rank 3) The blessing lasts for a long duration instead.

name: Wayfarer's Shield
effect:
You draw a circle of ash and oil around the company and ask the road to be gentle with them.
On a success, you ward yourself and up to six willing travelers for one day. Warded travelers gain +1 boon on rolls to endure heat, cold, storms, foul air, and other natural hardships of the environment, and harsh weather inflicts no Fatigue on them. When they camp, the ward holds wind, rain, and cold at bay, and they can take a night's rest in the open as if sheltered.
This ward offers no protection against creatures or violence, and none against weather or other hardships brought about by magic.
You can only cast this spell once per day.
heightened: (Rank 3) You can ward yourself and up to twelve willing travelers instead.

name: Dome of Sanctuary
effect:
You create a dome of translucent energy around the target location, emitting calm and peacefulness. The dome surrounds an area in melee range of the target location. You can choose which creatures are tolerated within the dome. This spell targets any creature not tolerated within the dome.
Weak. Deal +2 psychic damage (ignore AV) against every target.
Strong. Deal +4 psychic damage (ignore AV) against every target and they are pushed close outside of the dome.
Critical. Deal +6 psychic damage (ignore AV) against every target and they are pushed close outside of the dome and fall prone.
Any creature not tolerated in the dome trying to enter it from the outside must succeed on a Spirit + Fortitude roll vs. your Resist, otherwise they can’t enter.
Attacks targeting any creature inside the dome suffer +1 bane. The dome lasts for a short duration.

name: Slow
effect:
You partially immobilize a group of creatures. Choose a close area within range and target up to six creatures within the area.
On a success, any target is stunned for a short duration. At the end of their turns, they can roll Spirit + Fortitude to end the effect early.

name: Spell-breaking Wave
effect:
You send a wave of pacifying energy towards a creature, removing enchantments and breaking spell effects. Either choose one enchantment affecting them or choose one spell they are concentrating on or that has an effect that lasts for a duration.
On a success, the enchantment or spell effect ends.

name: Anti-Magic Field
effect:
You create a sphere of pure anti-magic.
On a success, you conjure the spherical field covering a close area around the target location. Within the field, all spellcasting or lasting magical effects are suppressed.
Any magical effect moving into the field is also immediately dispelled and magical area of effects exclude the space of the field.
The field lasts for a short duration.

name: Martyrdom's Blessing
effect:
You bestow a powerful blessing of self-sacrifice upon an ally or yourself.
On a success, the target gains the following benefits for a short duration:
- Whenever an ally within medium range would take damage, they can choose to take that damage instead. When taking damage for an ally this way, they gain resistance to that damage.
- After taking damage for at least one ally, the first attack roll on the target’s and that ally’s next turn both gain +1 boon.

name: Pacifying Presence
effect:
You emanate an overwhelming aura of serenity that subdues violent thoughts and actions. Choose up to eight creatures within range.
On a success, all targets suffer the following effects for a short duration:
- They cannot make attacks or cast spells dealing damage unless they succeed on a Spirit + Fortitude roll vs. your Resist.
- Whenever they deal damage to another creature, they take half that amount as psychic damage (ignore AV).
- This spell’s effect ends early when the target suffers any damage, unless it is from this spell’s effect.

name: Pilgrim's Gate
effect:
Over hours of consecration you anoint a doorway, arch, or gate with sacred oils, binding it in covenant to its one distant twin.
Each threshold is consecrated separately with its own casting of this spell, and each casting consumes 1500 coins worth of sacred oils and offerings.
On a success, the threshold is consecrated and bound to its twin. You can maintain at most one linked pair, and the two thresholds can lie at most seven days' march apart. Consecrating a new threshold beyond the pair causes your oldest to fade. While both thresholds remain intact, you can open the way as an Action while standing at either threshold and speaking the blessing. The way holds until the start of your next turn, and during that time any creature you permit may step through one threshold and out of the other. The consecration is a physical anointing. Defacing the threshold, breaking the doorway, or a single casting that ends spells destroys it, and destroying either threshold severs the covenant.
heightened: (Rank 5) Your thresholds can lie anywhere within the same sphere of existence with no limit on the distance between them, and you may maintain any number of consecrated thresholds at once. A creature stepping through one may step out of any other you choose. Each casting consumes 5000 coins worth of materials instead.

name: Redemptive Sacrifice
effect:
You open the wellspring of your own life and pour it out, giving freely what violence took.
On a success, sacrifice up to 10 of your own HP. Up to two other creatures in range each restore twice as many HP as you sacrificed. Any of them that are dying also stop dying and stabilize.
The HP you sacrifice cannot be redirected, prevented, or reduced.
heightened: (Rank 5) The targets each restore three times as many HP as you sacrificed instead.

name: Battle Surge
effect:
Heighten the senses of a creature in range, making it more fierce in battle.
Cast this spell during the target’s turn. As part of casting this spell, spend 2 HP. On a success, the target gains +1 Movement and +1 boon on their next attack roll during their current turn.

name: Mighty Strike
effect: Conjure raw spiritual strength into your weapon strike, letting divine force drive the blow instead of muscle.

name: Spectral Slash
effect:
Conjure a spectral blade that cuts at an enemy.
Weak. Deal +2 damage.
Strong. Deal +4 damage and the target suffers bleeding (2) until healed.
Critical. Deal +6 damage and the target suffers bleeding (4) until healed.

name: Righteous Strike
effect:
You name the wrong done and your weapon grows heavy with your god's answer.
Target only a creature that has dealt damage to you or one of your allies since the end of your last turn.
On a hit, judgement weighs on the target and they briefly suffer +1 bane on their next attack roll.

name: Victor's Shout
effect:
Cast this spell when an enemy within your sight is slain. You throw your weapon arm high and give the kill a voice.
On a success, each ally in short range of you may immediately end the frightened condition on themselves, and may immediately move a close distance for free.

name: Curse of War
effect:
You curse a creature with the magical power of war, suffocating them in the agony of battle.
On a success, the creature suffers the following effects:
- They take +2 damage from any source.
- Whenever they take 10 or more damage from one attack, they have to roll Spirit + Fortitude. On a failure, they are briefly frightened.
This curse lasts for a long duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The target takes +4 damage from any source.
(Rank 3) The target takes +6 damage from any source.

name: Heroic Weapon
effect:
Imbue a weapon with the power of courage. If the target is dual-wielding, you can instead target both their held weapons.
On a success, the weapon deals +2 damage. On a strong or critical hit, the attack‘s target is briefly frightened. Each creature can suffer the condition from this spell only once per scene. If you target your own weapon, you can make a weapon attack with it as part of casting this spell.
This spell lasts for a medium duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The weapon deals +4 damage instead.
(Rank 3) The weapon deals +6 damage instead.

name: Retaliating Fury
effect:
Your ancestors' fury guides your guard, and the answer follows the block like thunder follows lightning. Cast this spell when you are attacked vs. your Parry or Dodge.
On a success, you gain +2 to your Parry or Dodge against the triggering attack. If the triggering attacker is within your melee range and their attack fails, your ancestors' fury lashes out and they take 2 damage (ignore AV).

name: Tear Wound
effect:
You exploit open wounds, cuts, and bruises, making them cause the target immense pain. You can only target a creature that is below their max. HP with this spell.
Weak. Deal +4 damage.
Strong. Deal +8 damage and the target suffers bleeding (2) until healed.
Critical. Deal +12 damage and the target suffers bleeding (4) until healed.

name: War Cry
effect:
You roar with the voice of the bravest warrior spirits, shaking all enemies in range to their core.
On a success, each enemy is briefly frightened of you. Any creature can only be affected by this spell once per scene.

name: Weapon Spirit
effect:
To use this spell, you must first sacrifice a physical weapon along spiritual ingredients worth 100 coins in a ritual taking one exploration turn.
On a success, you summon an animated, spiritual version of one of the weapons you sacrificed for this spell levitating above the ground within range and obeying your command. The weapon can immediately make an attack against a target in range. Use your roll’s result for this attack. On each of your following turns, you can move the spiritual weapon a short distance within range and repeat the roll to make an attack with it.

name: Terror's Edge
effect:
Your weapon drinks the target's dread, and the edge arrives exactly where a shaking guard fails.
On a hit against a frightened target, terror seizes them and they treat their next Fear Check as if they had rolled a 1.
On a strong or critical hit against a frightened target, their terror also deepens: if they are briefly frightened, they are frightened for a short duration instead.

name: Avenging Oath
effect:
You point your weapon at the guilty and swear retribution before your ancestors, and a burning sigil of the broken shield settles over them.
Target one creature that has dealt damage to one of your allies this scene.
On a success, the sigil clings to them for a short duration, visible to all. Weapon attacks against them gain +1 boon.
They can roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the oath early.

name: War Banner
effect:
You drive one weapon you are holding into the ground and call your ancestors to stand where it stands.
On a success, the planted weapon becomes a war banner for a short duration. It is an object with 8 Parry, 8 Dodge, 6 AV, and 20 HP. Allies in close range of the banner cannot gain the frightened condition. When an ally in close range of the banner would be moved against their will or knocked prone, they can plant their feet and ignore it. Each ally can do this once per turn. Allies' weapon attacks against enemies in close range of the banner gain +1 boon.
The banner stands where planted and cannot be moved. If it is destroyed or you take it up again, the spell ends.
heightened: (Rank 3) The banner stands for a medium duration instead.

name: Ancestral Warriors
effect:
You conjure the memory of fallen ancestors around you. They appear as ghostly warriors of your chosen culture.
On a success, they swirl around you in close range. Any enemy in range takes +4 force damage when you cast this spell and at the start of each of their turns. The area in range is also considered difficult terrain for enemies.
This spell lasts for a short duration.

name: Blood Sacrifice
effect:
You cut into your own flesh, granting you the ancestor’s power in return. Cast this spell on your turn.
You lose 5 HP as part of casting this spell regardless of the result.
On a success, you gain the following effects:
- Gain +1 boon on weapon attacks.
- Add 2 x Mysticism to weapon damage.

name: Counterstroke
effect:
The war-spirits sharpen your eye until every opening in an enemy's guard shines like polished bronze.
On a success, you enter a vigilant stance for a short duration. When a creature in your melee range fails a melee attack against you, you can use your Quick Action to make an Opportunity Attack against them.
heightened:
(Rank 3) These Opportunity Attacks deal +4 damage.
(Rank 4) These Opportunity Attacks deal +6 damage instead.
(Rank 5) These Opportunity Attacks deal +8 damage instead.

name: Haste
effect:
You drastically heighten a creature’s senses, granting them supernatural speed at the cost of straining their body.
Choose a willing creature in range. On a success, the target gains the following effects:
- gain +1 Movement per turn
- gain +1 Parry and Dodge
- gain +1 boon on all Strength and Agility rolls
- you can replace your Quick Action on your turn for a second Action
The spell lasts for a short duration. When the spell ends, the target suffers one Fatigue and is briefly paralyzed.
heightened: (Rank 3) Choose a second target to also gain the spell‘s effects.

name: Commander's Shout
effect:
You cry out a battle-order that ripples down the line, and your allies surge forward as one.
On a success, each ally in range may immediately make one weapon or spell attack, or move a short distance. An ally that does so suffers 1 Fatigue.
Each creature can benefit from this spell only once per scene.
heightened:
(Rank 4) The spell affects allies in medium range instead.
(Rank 5) The spell affects allies in long range instead, and each affected ally gains +1 boon on the attack they make.

name: Righteous Fury
effect:
Divine wrath surges through you like molten bronze. Your eyes burn with holy fury and the unjust tremble before your strikes.
On a success, for a short duration you gain the following effects:
- Your weapon attacks deal +4 damage.
- Your weapon attacks against frightened creatures gain +1 boon and deal +4 additional damage.
- You are immune to the frightened condition.
heightened:
(Rank 4) Your weapon attacks deal +6 damage, and +6 additional damage against frightened creatures, instead.
(Rank 5) Your weapon attacks deal +8 damage, and +8 additional damage against frightened creatures, instead.

name: Glorious Strike
effect:
You bring your weapon down wreathed in golden war-fire, and the proud blow lays low any who dared stand against you.
On a hit, add the following fire damage:
Weak. +4 fire damage.
Strong. +8 fire damage, and the target is briefly frightened of you.
Critical. +12 fire damage, and the target is frightened of you for a short duration.
heightened:
(Rank 4) Deal +6/+12/+18 fire damage instead.
(Rank 5) Deal +8/+16/+24 fire damage instead.

name: Ancestral Guardian
effect:
You call the spirits of fallen warriors, and a spectral champion rises to stand watch, shield raised and blade ready.
On a success, summon a spectral guardian at a location in range for a short duration. It acts as a bulwark under your control with the following properties:
- The guardian has 30 HP, 4 AV, and uses your Resist for all its Defenses. It is immune to all conditions.
- It holds its space and cannot be moved by enemies. On each of your turns, you can move it a short distance.
- Guard. Once per round, when a creature adjacent to the guardian is hit by an attack, you can have the guardian intercept the blow. The guardian takes the damage instead, reduced by its AV.
- Strike. On your turn, you can spend your Quick Action to have the guardian attack. Roll Spirit + Mysticism vs. Parry of a target in reach of it. On a hit, it deals +4/+8/+12 force damage.
The guardian remains for a short duration, until you dismiss it, or until its HP are reduced to 0.
heightened:
(Rank 4) The guardian has 40 HP and 6 AV, its Strike deals +6/+12/+18 force damage, and it can use Guard twice per round, instead.
(Rank 5) The guardian has 50 HP and 8 AV, its Strike deals +8/+16/+24 force damage, and it can use Guard three times per round, instead.

name: Blood Frenzy
effect:
You open your own veins to the war-spirits and let the red song take you.
On a success, you enter a frenzy for a short duration and gain the following effects:
- At the start of each of your turns, you take 2 damage (ignore AV) as the frenzy feeds on you.
- You gain +1 Movement per turn.
- On a strong or critical hit, your weapon attacks inflict bleeding (4).
- When you hit a bleeding creature with a weapon attack, their wounds tear wider and their bleeding increases by 2.
- You are immune to the frightened condition.
- Whenever you reduce an enemy to 0 HP, each of their allies in close range of them with the same or a lower tier than the killed creature must roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist or be briefly frightened of you.
The frenzy lasts for a short duration. You can immediately recast this spell as an Action at the end of the duration to remain in the frenzy for a longer time.
heightened: (Rank 5) Your weapon attacks inflict bleeding (6) on a strong or critical hit instead.

name: Champion's Challenge
effect:
You strike your weapon against your chest and name your foe, and a burning sigil seals the duel between you.
Target one creature of a tier equal to or lower than your Mysticism.
On a success, the sigil binds you both for a short duration and the target suffers the following effects:
- They suffer +1 bane on attacks against anyone other than you.
- When they deal damage to anyone other than you, the sigil sears them for 6 damage (ignore AV).
- They cannot benefit from boons granted by their allies on rolls against you.
- Your weapon attacks against them gain +1 boon.
- If they end their turn without you in their sight or beyond medium range of you, the sigil brands their cowardice and they are frightened of you for a short duration.
They can roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the duel early.

name: Siege Breaker
effect:
You bring your weapon down with the weight of a falling gate, and walls learn to fear you.
On a hit, add the following damage:
Weak. +4 damage.
Strong. +8 damage.
Critical. +12 damage.
Also on a hit, the target loses 3 AV (min. 0) for a short duration.
Against objects, structures, and stationary barriers, this attack deals double damage, and it destroys any mundane cover it strikes.
heightened: (Rank 5) Add +6/+12/+18 damage and the target loses 4 AV (min. 0) instead.

name: Illuminated Script
effect:
You hold light over a text and pray for revelation. The truth of the words rises glowing from the surface. Target one text, inscription, or set of markings you study during the ritual.
Weak. You learn the text's general subject and the intent of its author.
Strong. You understand the text's plain meaning in full, as if fluent in its language.
Critical. As strong, and the light reveals whether hidden layers exist, such as ciphers, concealed messages, or magical obfuscation, and you gain +1 boon on rolls to uncover them.
You can only cast this spell once per text.

name: Deepening Venom
effect:
You whisper to the venom already in the target's blood, and it wakes, virulent and patient.
Target one creature that is currently poisoned.
Weak. Deal +4 poison damage.
Strong. Deal +8 poison damage.
Critical. Deal +12 poison damage.
On any success, the target also suffers 4 lasting poison damage while they remain poisoned.
heightened:
(Rank 3) Deal +6/+12/+18 poison damage and inflict 6 lasting poison damage instead.
(Rank 4) Deal +8/+16/+24 poison damage and inflict 8 lasting poison damage instead.
(Rank 5) Deal +10/+20/+30 poison damage and inflict 10 lasting poison damage instead.

name: Gift of the Wild
effect:
You call on the beasts of deep water, sheer cliff, and high grass, reshaping a creature's body with their gifts. Target one willing creature you touch and choose one of the following:
- Gills (the target can breathe underwater and gains +1 boon on Athletics rolls for swimming)
- Spiderlimbs (the target can attempt to climb sheer, overhanging, or ceiling surfaces that would normally be impossible, climbs at full Movement, and gains +1 boon on Athletics rolls for climbing)
- Springlegs (the target's jumping distances are doubled and they gain +1 boon on Athletics rolls for jumping)
On a success, the target gains the chosen gift for a medium duration. A creature can only benefit from one gift at a time. A new casting replaces the old.
heightened:
(Rank 3) Target up to three willing creatures you touch instead, all gaining the same chosen gift.
(Rank 4) Up to five creatures instead.
(Rank 5) Up to seven creatures instead.

name: Augury
effect:
You cast bones beneath the night sky, read the drift of smoke, or trace the pattern of stars, seeking an omen for what is to come. Describe one specific course of action you intend to take within the coming hour.
On a success, you receive an omen of its likely outcome, one of the following: favorable, unfavorable, mixed (both good and bad are likely to come of it), or nothing (no strong outcome either way). The omen does not account for choices or events that might later change the outcome.
Further castings about the same course of action show the same omen until circumstances meaningfully change.

name: Divination
effect:
You burn a precious offering under the open night and pose your question to the powers beyond the veil of stars. Ask one question about a specific goal, event, or activity set to occur or conclude within the next seven days.
On a success, the powers answer truthfully. The GM gives you one truthful insight for a weak success, two for a strong success, and three for a critical success, drawn from the options below (or fewer if fewer meaningfully apply). Each insight is a short, plain statement.
- The most likely outcome of the course of action.
- The single greatest obstacle standing in its way.
- The single most helpful ally, action, or resource toward it.
- A symbolic omen or portent, given when the outcome is genuinely uncertain or hidden even from the powers.
The answer does not account for choices or events that might later change the outcome. Asking again about the same matter yields the same insights until circumstances meaningfully change.

name: Moonlit Mirror
properties: ritual (minutes), material (mirror or vessel of still water), concentrate
effect:
Under open sky or candlelight you tilt a reflecting surface toward the unseen, and the moon's pale light parts the distance between you and the one you seek. Target one creature you know, anywhere in the same sphere of existence. Unwilling creatures roll their Resist against your casting. You must channel the vision through a connection to the target: a possession of theirs, a part of their body, or deep personal familiarity. Without any such connection, suffer +2 banes.
On a success, the surface shows the target and their immediate surroundings for a short duration while you concentrate.
Weak. The vision is hazy and silent. You make out general surroundings and activity, but no fine detail.
Strong. The vision is clear. You recognize faces and objects and hear muffled sound.
Critical. The vision is vivid with clear sound, and you sense whether any magic conceals, protects, or misleads what you see.
A scried creature with any magical senses can roll Spirit + Perception vs. your Resist to feel that they are being watched.

name: Warded Threshold
effect: You lay your hand on a threshold and speak a vow of sanctuary, and a blessing settles over it that turns away all who would force their way through. Target one closed door, window, gate, chest, lid, or other entryway no larger than a large Size that you touch. On a success, it is held fast and counts as a magically sealed fastening. It cannot be opened, picked, or forced by nonmagical means while the blessing holds. When you cast this spell, you may name any creatures who can still open the portal freely. A Knock spell suppresses the blessing for a short duration, during which the portal can be opened and shut normally, and any effect that ends spells removes it. The blessing lasts for a long duration.
heightened:
(Rank 3) Choose one of the benefits below when you cast the spell.
(Rank 4) Choose two instead.
(Rank 5) Choose three instead.
- You sense it the moment any creature touches the warded portal, even while asleep, as long as you are within an hour's travel of it.
- The first creature to force the portal is briefly dazed as the sanctuary turns their will aside.
- You bless one additional portal you touch during the casting with the same effect.
- The blessing lasts for a very long duration instead of a long one.

name: Updraft
effect:
You call up a sudden column of rushing air that swells beneath a falling body and bears it gently down. Cast this spell as a Quick Action when you or a creature you can see within range falls.
On a success, the target rides the updraft softly downward a short distance per round instead of falling and takes no falling damage when they land. On the way down they can drift up to a short distance horizontally on the wind. The effect lasts for a short duration. If it ends while the target is still airborne, they fall the remaining distance as normal.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Affect up to two falling creatures within range instead.
(Rank 3) Up to three creatures instead.
(Rank 4) Up to four creatures instead.
(Rank 5) Up to five creatures instead.

name: Sleep
effect: You breathe out a soft dream-mist that settles over a close area within range, and eyelids grow heavy. Target each hostile creature in the area whose tier is equal to or lower than your Mysticism. On a success, each target falls unconscious for a short duration. A sleeping creature wakes and the condition ends immediately if they take any damage or suffer any other physical disturbance, such as being shaken, shoved, or moved, or if an adjacent creature spends an Action to rouse them. Loud noise alone does not wake them. Creatures that do not sleep, such as most undead and constructs, are unaffected.
heightened:
(Rank 3) The sleep lasts for a medium duration instead.
(Rank 4) The sleep lasts for a medium duration and the dream-mist covers a short area instead.
(Rank 5) The sleep lasts for a long duration and the dream-mist covers a medium area instead.

name: Zone of Truth
effect: You consecrate a close area within range with a clear, revealing radiance in which falsehood cannot hold. Target each creature in the area. Each affected creature knows they are being compelled. While an affected creature remains in the area, they cannot speak a deliberate lie. They can still stay silent, give a short or evasive answer, or say what they believe to be true even if they are mistaken. A creature that leaves the area is no longer affected. A creature that enters the area after the spell is cast is targeted when they enter, using your roll’s result vs. their Resist. The zone lasts for a medium duration.
heightened: (Rank 4) The zone covers a short area and lasts for a long duration instead.

name: Serene Exile
effect: You speak a word of stillness and the target vanishes from sight. Target one creature of a tier equal to or lower than your Mysticism. On a success, the target is drawn out of the world and into a serene pocket realm beyond it while you concentrate, up to a short duration. While set aside, they cannot act, move, be targeted, or affect anything in the world, and nothing in the world can affect them. When the spell ends, the target returns to the space they left, or the nearest open space if that space is occupied. If the target is a conjured or extraplanar creature and you concentrate for the full short duration, they are instead sent to their native sphere of existence and any effect summoning or binding them ends.

name: Diminish
effect:
You point at a creature and draw the vigor from their frame until their body dwindles. Target one creature within range. Against a willing creature, roll vs. moderate difficulty (8). Against an unwilling creature, roll vs. their Resist. On a success, the target’s body shrinks one Size smaller along with their worn equipment and held items, for a short duration. While diminished by this spell, they suffer the following effects.
- They suffer +1 bane on Strength rolls.
- They suffer -1 Parry and gain +1 Dodge.
- They lose 1 AV (min. 0).
- They deal -2 damage with Strength-based attacks (min. 0).
heightened: (Rank 3) The target suffers the following effects instead. They suffer +1 bane on Strength rolls, suffer -2 Parry and gain +2 Dodge, lose 2 AV (min. 0), and deal -4 damage with Strength-based attacks (min. 0).

name: Gaseous Form
effect:
Your body unravels into a slow coil of shadowy vapor, cool and near-formless. On a success, you take gaseous form for a medium duration while you concentrate. While in this form you gain the following.
- You can slip through any gap or crack that is not airtight, and you hover, moving up to a close distance in any direction each turn.
- You have resistance to physical damage and you cannot be grappled, restrained, or knocked prone.
- You cannot attack, cast spells, or manipulate objects, and you cannot enter water or other liquid.
- Strong wind pushes you as it would smoke, and you are pushed a short distance by any effect that would move you.
You can end the spell at will, returning to your normal shape in an open space you occupy.
heightened:
(Rank 4) You can instead target one willing creature you touch, or the form lasts for a long duration.
(Rank 5) You can target up to three willing creatures you touch, and the form lasts for a long duration.

name: Revivify
effect:
You pour life force into a body whose soul still lingers at the threshold, calling it back before it crosses. Target a creature that died since the end of your last turn, or within the last minute outside of combat. The casting consumes 500 coins worth of sacred oils and offerings whether or not it succeeds.
On a success, the creature returns to life with 1 HP and suffers one Wound from the trauma of death. On a failure, their soul slips too far for swift recall. They can still be returned by a Raise Dead or Resurrection spell.

name: Raise Dead
effect:
Through hours of rites you call a soul back along the fading path to its body. Target the body of a creature that died within the last seven days. The body must be whole. If it is missing crucial parts or is too badly destroyed, only a Resurrection spell can return them. The ritual consumes 1500 coins worth of offerings and incense whether or not it succeeds. Roll vs. TN 14.
On a success, the creature returns to life with 1 HP. They suffer 2 Fatigue that cannot be removed until they complete a full rest, and all Wounds they had when they died remain. On a failure, the soul cannot be called back by this spell again. They can still be returned by a Resurrection spell.

name: Resurrection
effect: Over hours of ritual and prayer you call a departed soul back across the threshold of death and bind it once more into flesh. You must have the creature’s physical remains, whether a whole body or only the ashes left by a spell such as Disintegrate, and the creature must have been dead no longer than one moon. The ritual consumes 5000 coins worth of rare offerings and incense whether or not it succeeds. Roll vs. TN 16. On a success, the creature is restored to a whole living body with half their maximum HP and all their Wounds healed. They suffer 2 Fatigue that cannot be removed until they complete a full rest. On a failure, the soul slips beyond reach and this creature cannot be the target of another Resurrection for a long duration.

name: Curse of Frailty
effect:
You curse a creature with the brittleness of old bones, and their body begins to fail them like a corpse rehearsing.
On a success, the creature suffers the following effects:
- They lose 2 AV (min. 0).
- They suffer +1 bane on Strength rolls.
This curse lasts for a long duration.
heightened:
(Rank 3) They lose 3 AV (min. 0) instead.
(Rank 4) They lose 4 AV (min. 0) instead.
(Rank 5) They lose 5 AV (min. 0) instead.

name: Grave Chill
effect:
The cold of the grave seeps into the target's marrow, and their body begins answering to the world below before their time.
On a success, the target is slowed for a short duration, and they suffer +1 bane on rolls to resist or end early the effects of Death spells, including this one. Whenever they fail a roll to end a Death spell early, they take 2 frost damage (ignore AV) as the grave's cold pulls at them. They can roll Strength + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end this spell's effects early.
heightened:
(Rank 3) They take 4 frost damage (ignore AV) whenever they fail a roll to end a Death spell early instead.
(Rank 4) They take 6 frost damage (ignore AV) whenever they fail a roll to end a Death spell early instead.
(Rank 5) They take 8 frost damage (ignore AV) whenever they fail a roll to end a Death spell early instead.

name: Weight of Dooms
effect:
You lay your will upon a doom already written on the target, and it sinks deeper, like a wound the body refuses to close.
Choose one condition the target currently suffers among poisoned, frightened, bleeding, slowed, dazed, or staggered.
Weak. The target suffers +1 bane on rolls to end the chosen condition early, and if the condition has a set duration, it extends by one step.
Strong. Same as a weak success, and deal +4 necrotic damage.
Critical. Same as a weak success, and deal +8 necrotic damage.
This spell has no effect on a target that suffers none of the listed conditions.
heightened:
(Rank 3) Deal +6/+12 necrotic damage on a strong or critical success instead.
(Rank 4) Deal +8/+16 necrotic damage on a strong or critical success instead.
(Rank 5) Deal +10/+20 necrotic damage on a strong or critical success instead.

name: Spreading Contagion
effect:
You breathe on an existing sickness and it wakes with a will of its own, leaping from body to body like sparks through dry grass.
Target one creature that is currently poisoned.
Weak. Deal +4 poison damage to the target.
Strong. Deal +8 poison damage to the target.
Critical. Deal +12 poison damage to the target.
Also target each enemy in close range of the first target, using your roll's result vs. their Resist. On a success, they become poisoned for a short duration.
This spell has no effect on a target that is not poisoned.
heightened:
(Rank 4) Deal +5/+10/+15 poison damage instead.
(Rank 5) Deal +6/+12/+18 poison damage instead.

name: The Reckoning
effect:
You speak the target's accumulated dooms aloud, and every sickness, terror, and curse upon them comes due at once, called home by the world below.
Count the target's afflictions: each condition they currently suffer among poisoned, frightened, bleeding, slowed, dazed, staggered, and stunned, and each curse spell affecting them.
Weak. Deal +2 necrotic damage, plus +4 necrotic damage for each affliction counted.
Strong. Deal +4 necrotic damage, plus +4 necrotic damage for each affliction counted.
Critical. Deal +6 necrotic damage, plus +4 necrotic damage for each affliction counted.
Also on a success, all counted conditions on the target end and all counted curse spells on them are lifted. The tally consumes what it counts.
heightened:
(Rank 4) Deal +5 necrotic damage for each affliction counted instead.
(Rank 5) Deal +6 necrotic damage for each affliction counted instead.
