name: Pestilence
effect:
You breathe out a rolling shroud of grave-air, and where it settles, flesh remembers it is mortal.
On a success, the plague fills the area in short range of the target location for up to a medium duration while you concentrate. The miasma is a lightly obscured area. Living creatures inside the area suffer the following effects:
- They are poisoned while inside the area.
- They cannot regain HP.
- They take 6 necrotic damage (ignore AV) at the start of each of their turns.
A creature that leaves the area remains briefly poisoned.
heightened: (Rank 5) The plague fills the area in medium range of the target location, and creatures inside take 8 necrotic damage (ignore AV) at the start of each of their turns, instead.

name: Fangs of the Dark
effect:
The shadows nearest the target grow teeth, and for a heartbeat the night itself feeds.
Weak. Deal +4 frost damage.
Strong. Deal +8 frost damage.
Critical. Deal +12 frost damage.
If the target is in dim light or darkness, or cannot see you, they take +6 additional frost damage and are briefly frightened as the dark closes its jaws.
heightened:
(Rank 3) Deal +6/+12/+18 frost damage, and the additional damage is +8 instead.
(Rank 4) Deal +8/+16/+24 frost damage, and the additional damage is +10 instead.
(Rank 5) Deal +10/+20/+30 frost damage, and the additional damage is +12 instead.

name: Shadow Mantle
effect:
You peel a creature's shadow up from the ground and drape it over their shoulders, where it clings like a second skin of night.
On a success, choose one creature in range. While they are in dim light or darkness:
- Attacks against them suffer +1 bane.
- Their attacks against creatures that cannot see them deal +2 damage.
The mantle lasts for a medium duration. If the wearer spends a full turn in bright light, it burns away and the spell ends.
heightened:
(Rank 3) Their attacks against creatures that cannot see them deal +4 damage instead.
(Rank 4) Their attacks against creatures that cannot see them deal +6 damage instead.
(Rank 5) Their attacks against creatures that cannot see them deal +8 damage instead.

name: Written in the Stars
effect:
You read the target's death in the night sky and pronounce it aloud, and for a moment everyone present can feel where the blow is fated to land.
On a success, the target is marked (4) for a short duration. The attack that ends the mark has its SL increased by one step (max. critical success).
heightened:
(Rank 3) The target is marked (6) instead.
(Rank 4) The target is marked (8) instead.
(Rank 5) The target is marked (10) instead.

name: Primal Guardian
effect:
You press your palms to the earth and call, and the land answers: a hulking guardian of gnarled root, packed soil, and living stone rises to stand with you.
On a success, the guardian rises from an unoccupied space of earth, stone, or living soil at the target location and acts as a creature under your control (treat them as a companion for combat). The guardian has the statistics of a Tier 4 companion with large Size and the Earth Elemental trait (see the Spirit (Primordial) companion traits under Mounts & Companions). In addition, apply the following changes:
- Living roots and green growth wind through the guardian's stone. They gain weakness to fire instead of blast.
- Grasping Roots. On a strong or critical hit with their Slam attack, the target is also grappled by coiling roots. The roots hold the target without binding the guardian. The grappled creature can use their Action to roll Strength or Agility + Athletics vs. the guardian's Parry. On a success, they tear free. The roots wither and release them if the guardian is reduced to 0 HP.
- Lashing Roots (crush). This attack replaces the guardian's Earth Spikes. Thorned roots whip out in all directions. Target all creatures in melee range. On a hit, subtract the weapon damage once from the total damage against each target. On a strong or critical hit, each target is briefly slowed by the tangling growth.
- Warden's Ground. The ground in close range of the guardian churns with shifting stone and grasping roots and counts as difficult terrain for your enemies.
The guardian remains for a short duration, until dismissed by you as a Quick Action, or until their HP are reduced to 0, after which they crumble back into soil and stone.
heightened: (Rank 5) The guardian has the statistics of a Tier 5 companion instead and can rise with up to huge Size.

name: Verdant Restoration
effect:
You bury a seed in a whispered blessing, and the ground answers with a sudden garden: flowering vines and healing herbs unfurl in moments, breathing their medicine into the air.
On a success, verdant regrowth blooms in short range of the target location. While the flowers hold, apply the following effects:
- Allies within the zone restore 4 HP at the start of each of their turns.
- Allies within the zone are immune to being poisoned and bleeding as the herbs' medicine fills their blood.
- The growth stands thick and high. Ranged attacks against allies within the zone suffer +1 bane.
The zone remains for a short duration, even if you move out of range of the area. The blooming plants need living ground and cannot take root on bare stone, worked floors, or sand.
heightened: (Rank 5) Allies within the zone restore 6 HP at the start of each of their turns instead.

name: Greater Restoration
effect:
You lay both hands on the afflicted and pour a flood of pure vitality through them, driving out everything that does not belong in a living body.
On a success, the target is cleansed of bodily afflictions. Apply the following effects:
- Heal up to two Wounds from the target. A creature can only benefit from one Wound-healing spell effect per day.
- The target is cured of any disease and any poison in their body, and the poisoned condition ends.
- Any effect that temporarily reduces an Attribute die of the target ends.
This spell restores the body only. It cannot lift curses or return the dead to life.
heightened: (Rank 5) Heal up to three Wounds from the target instead.

name: Life Ward
effect:
You seal a reserve of life force beneath the target's skin, a quiet ember that waits for the moment their own strength fails.
Target one willing creature.
On a success, the target is warded for a long duration. While the ward holds, when the target would be reduced to 0 HP, they remain at 1 HP instead and the ward's stored vitality restores +4 HP to them. Then the ward ends in a flash of warm light.
A creature can only benefit from this spell once per day.
heightened: (Rank 5) Target up to two willing creatures instead.

name: Dreamwalking
effect:
You lie down beneath the veil of sleep and step sideways into the dreaming, following a silver thread to another sleeper's dream. Target one sleeping creature you know, anywhere in the same sphere of existence. You must channel the walk through a connection to the target: a possession of theirs, a part of their body, or deep personal familiarity. Without such a connection, you cannot cast this spell. If the target is not asleep, the spell automatically fails.
On a success, you enter the target's dream for a short duration while you concentrate. Against an unwilling dreamer, your roll must also beat their Resist to enter their dream. Within the dream you can do the following:
- Observe. The dream's shape reveals what occupies the sleeper. The GM chooses what the dream shows from the following: a present fear, a desire or ambition, a face or place that fills their thoughts, or a symbolic image when nothing concrete surfaces.
- Converse. You appear within the dream in any guise you choose and can speak with the dreamer. They can answer, refuse, or turn away as they choose. Upon waking, they remember the exchange as an unusually vivid dream.
- Suggest. You plant a single thought of up to 25 words that feels like the dreamer's own upon waking, using your roll's result vs. their Resist. The thought can incline them toward a course of action they might plausibly consider. It is a nudge, never a command, and it fades after one day if not acted on.
If the sleeper wakes, the spell ends immediately. Nothing within the dream can harm you or the dreamer.

name: Eclipse
effect:
You speak the moon's oldest claim, and for a stretch of the world the day is unmade: cold, starless night falls in full.
On a success, unnatural night swallows the area in short range of the target location for a short duration while you concentrate. Apply the following effects:
- The area counts as total darkness and any light source within it immediately goes out.
- You alone see through the eclipse's darkness as if it were dim light, guided by the hidden moon.
- Any hostile creature that starts their turn within the area takes 6 frost damage (ignore AV) as the dark's cold seeps into them and must roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist. On a failure, they are briefly frightened of the surrounding darkness. A creature that succeeds on this roll is immune to this spell's fear effect until the spell ends.
heightened: (Rank 5) The night swallows the area in medium range of the target location instead and the frost damage increases to 8 (ignore AV).

name: Prophecy
effect:
Through a night-long trance beneath the open stars you let the threads of fate run through your hands, and what is to come brushes against you in signs and visions. Name one person, place, or undertaking. The trance consumes 1500 coins worth of rare incense and offerings whether or not it succeeds.
On a success, you receive visions of what may come to pass for the named matter within the next season. The GM gives you one vision for a weak success, two for a strong success, and three for a critical success, each drawn from the following (or fewer if fewer meaningfully apply):
- A danger that approaches the named matter, shown by one telling quality you will know when you meet it, such as a sound, a smell, or a repeated sigil.
- A hand behind events, appearing as a shadow or masked figure that openly wears one recognizable detail, such as a scarred hand, a distinctive ring, or the cadence of a voice.
- A place or time that will prove decisive, marked by a telling feature of the land or the stand of sun, moon, or stars.
- A symbolic omen, given when the future is genuinely unformed or hidden even from the stars.
Each vision is a brief symbolic scene, never plain instruction, and reveals no identity beyond its telling detail. You know a vision has come to pass the moment you perceive its detail before you, never earlier. When that happens, you cannot be surprised in that moment and you gain +1 boon on the first roll you make in response.
The visions do not account for choices or events that might later change what comes. Casting this spell again about the same matter shows the same visions until circumstances meaningfully change.

name: Veil of Secrets
effect:
You draw the dusk over a place like a curtain, and what happens within slips out of the world's sight and memory.
On a success, a veil settles over the area in short range of the target location for a medium duration while you concentrate. Apply the following effects:
- To any observer outside, the area appears still and empty to every sense, as it would seem with no one within it. Creatures and objects inside cannot be perceived from outside, and no sound, smell, light, or smoke from within crosses the veil's edge.
- Any scrying sensor or divination magic targeting the area or anything inside it perceives only the same empty stillness.
- Creatures inside the veil perceive their surroundings and the world outside normally.
The veil does not bar passage. Any creature can walk in, and once inside they perceive everything within normally. An outside observer with reason to be suspicious of the place can roll Spirit + Perception vs. your Resist to sense that the stillness is unnatural, though not what it hides.
heightened: (Rank 5) The veil extends in medium range of the target location instead and lasts for a long duration.

name: Avatar of War
effect:
You call down the war-spirits of your ancestors and they answer in your flesh, and you rise taller, wreathed in the fury of every battle ever won.
On a success, you become an avatar of war for a short duration and gain the following effects:
- At the start of each of your turns, the war-fury feeds on you and you take 4 damage (ignore AV).
- You are immune to the frightened condition and cannot be knocked prone.
- You gain +1 Movement per turn.
- Your weapon attacks gain +1 boon and deal +8 additional damage against frightened creatures.
- At the start of each of your turns, roll Spirit + Mysticism vs. Resist against all enemies in close range of you. On a success, that creature is briefly frightened of you. On a failure, that creature is immune to this fear effect for a short duration.
This form lasts for a short duration. You can immediately recast this spell as an Action at the end of the duration to remain an avatar of war for a longer time.

name: Final Triumph
effect:
The war-spirits seize upon the kill and shriek the victory across the field, and every warrior present feels the battle turn on its hinge.
Cast this spell as a Quick Action when you or an ally within range reduces an enemy to 0 HP.
On a success, apply the following effects:
- Target each enemy in range who could see the fallen and whose Tier is equal to or lower than the fallen creature's Tier, using your roll's result vs. their Resist. On a success, that creature is frightened of the fallen's killer for a short duration.
- Each ally in range takes heart. They shed the frightened condition, are immune to being frightened for a short duration, and gain +1 boon on their next roll.

name: Divine Retribution
effect:
You raise your weapon to the sky and call down judgement, and a pillar of golden fire falls upon the guilty like the wrath of every wronged ancestor.
Weak. Deal +8 fire damage.
Strong. Deal +16 fire damage.
Critical. Deal +24 fire damage.
If the target is frightened, the judgement hammers their broken nerve and they are briefly stunned.
If the target has reduced one of your allies to 0 HP this scene, deal +12/+24/+36 fire damage instead as retribution.

name: Undying Devotion
effect:
You speak the names of your companions as a vow, and a thread of your own life ties itself to each of their hearts.
On a success, choose up to four allies in range. A thread of devotion binds each of them to you for a medium duration while you concentrate. Once bound, the threads stretch up to long range. While a bound ally is beyond long range of you, their thread grows slack and grants none of these effects until they return.
While the threads hold, you gain the following effects:
- Whenever a bound ally takes damage, you can choose to suffer it in their stead (ignore AV). They take no damage.
- Whenever you take damage in an ally's stead, through this or any other of your *Peace* spells, you only take half of it (rounded up).
- The first time each bound ally would be reduced to 0 HP, they remain at 1 HP instead and their thread burns away. You take 12 damage (ignore AV). This damage cannot be redirected, prevented, or reduced.
If you are reduced to 0 HP, this spell ends immediately.

name: Sanctuary Sphere
effect:
You raise a wide sphere of hallowed peace, a truce sworn in the presence of the gods. Within its bounds no hand can be raised in anger, and death itself waits at the threshold.
On a success, the sanctuary fills the area in short range of the target location for a medium duration while you concentrate. Any creature can enter or leave the sanctuary freely.
While the sanctuary holds, apply the following effects on all creatures, including your allies:
- Creatures inside the sanctuary cannot attack or cast spells that deal damage. The peace allows no roll against it.
- Attacks and spells dealing damage cannot cross the sanctuary's edge. Creatures outside cannot harm anyone inside, and creatures inside cannot harm anyone outside.
- A creature inside the sanctuary sheds the frightened condition and cannot gain it, and any recurring damage they suffer from ongoing effects, such as burning, bleeding, or poison, is suppressed while they remain inside.
- A dying creature inside the sanctuary does not count down their remaining dying turns. Death waits until the sanctuary ends or they are carried beyond its edge.
The sanctuary can only be broken early by an effect that ends spells, and only if that effect is cast at rank 5.

name: Sacred Covenant
effect:
Over hours of rite and offering you bind a place to the law of the gods, anointing its threshold and its stones with oil, ash, and the blood of a sacrificed bull. The rite consumes 5000 coins worth of oils, offerings, and sacrifices whether or not it succeeds.
On a success, the covenant settles over a single temple, palace, village, or other bounded site up to a thousand paces across, and holds for one season. You may maintain only one Sacred Covenant at a time. During the rite, you may name any number of keepers of the ground. While the covenant holds, apply the following effects within the covenant ground:
- When a creature freely swears an oath aloud within the ground, the covenant seals it before the gods. Any creature who breaks a sealed oath is branded with a sigil of broken faith, plain to see upon their brow, and suffers +1 bane on all rolls while the brand lasts. The brand only fades when they atone for the breaking at the covenant ground.
- When a creature sheds blood within the ground or defiles its stones and altars, the wrath of the covenant strikes them for 8 psychic damage (ignore AV) and they are branded with the sigil of broken faith as if they had broken a sealed oath.
- When a creature makes an attack within the ground, they must roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist or be paralyzed for a short duration as the weight of the covenant presses down on them. They can repeat the roll at the end of their turns to end the effect early.
- You and the named keepers know the moment the covenant ground is defiled by bloodshed, desecration, or the breaking of a sealed oath, wherever you are within the same sphere of existence, and you know which of the three it was.
If you recast this spell upon the same ground each time the season turns, four seasons in a row, the covenant becomes permanent. A permanent covenant needs no further renewal and no longer counts as the one covenant you may maintain.
The covenant can be undone by a rite of equal weight, by the ground's destruction, or by an effect that ends spells, but only if that effect is cast at rank 5.

name: The Pilgrim's Road
effect:
Through a night-long rite you consecrate the road ahead, anointing staff and sandal and naming the journey's end aloud before the gods, and the way opens itself to the faithful. The rite consumes 5000 coins worth of blessed oils and offerings whether or not it succeeds.
Name a destination you know of and whose location you know, even if you have never walked the way there. On a success, you bless the journey for yourself and up to twelve willing travelers with you. The blessing holds until the company reaches the destination, forsakes the journey, or one moon passes, whichever comes first. While the blessing holds, apply the following effects:
- The road reveals itself. The company always knows the truest way toward the destination, even through lands unknown to them, and cannot become lost.
- The company travels with supernatural swiftness, tireless as the road itself. The group gains +2 progress on each day of travel, rolls to travel the blessed route gain +1 boon, and no traveler gains Fatigue from the journey itself.
- The road wards its pilgrims from foul weather along the way, and harsh weather inflicts no Fatigue on them.
- When a creature makes an attack against a blessed traveler on the road, the attacker must roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist or be dazed for a short duration.
The road follows the living land and does not pierce it. It is no portal, and the journey is still made step by step.

name: Dawnbreak
effect:
You raise your hands to the dark sky and speak the sun's true name, and dawn breaks hours before its hour, flooding the land in the pale gold of the risen day.
On a success, the light of true dawn fills the land within a thousand paces of the target location and holds for a medium duration while you concentrate. Cast during the night, it brings the day before its hour, bright as the risen sun. Cast during the day, it holds the daylight unbroken against all that would dim or darken it. The daylight fills every space within its reach, under open sky or beneath stone, and no wall, roof, or depth bars the risen sun. Within the daylight, apply the following effects:
- Any area of magical darkness is dispelled, and any spell sustaining darkness, night, or shadow over the land ends.
- The land is filled with bright light, and no creature within it can be hidden or invisible.
- Undead creatures are cowed by the risen sun and suffer +1 bane on all rolls while in the daylight.
- You and your allies gain +1 boon on rolls to cast light spells within the daylight.
- Allies cannot be frightened, and any frightened condition on them ends.

name: Final Judgement
effect:
You pronounce the last sentence of the light, and a blade of pure dawn falls upon the condemned.
You can target a hidden or invisible creature with this spell if you are aware of their presence, and your roll suffers no banes from being unable to see them.
Weak. Deal +8 radiant damage.
Strong. Deal +16 radiant damage.
Critical. Deal +24 radiant damage.
If the target is blinded, burning, or invisible, deal +12/+24/+36 radiant damage instead.
An undead creature slain by this spell is destroyed utterly, their remains burned to ash, and they cannot be raised or reanimated again.

name: Everlasting Night
effect:
Through a night-long rite you raise a stele of black stone and speak the moon's claim over the land, and the sun forgets the way there. The rite consumes rare offerings and the graven stele worth 5000 coins, whether or not it succeeds.
On a success, unending night settles over a single valley, city, or other bounded site up to a thousand paces across, centered on the anchor stele, and holds for one moon. You may maintain only one Everlasting Night at a time, and you can renew it by casting this spell again at the stele before the moon turns. Within the site, apply the following effects:
- The sun does not rise over the site. Under open sky the land is lit as by a clear, moonlit night, dim light at its brightest hour. Mundane fires, lamps, and magical light sources work normally within the night.
- Spells and abilities that require night, darkness, or moonlight treat the whole site as night at all hours.
- You and any creatures you name during the rite see within the site's night as if it were bright light.
The anchor stele is an object with 8 AV and 30 HP. If it is destroyed, the night lifts at once. The night also ends if an effect that ends spells, cast at rank 5, is used upon the stele, or when the light of a *Dawnbreak* fills the site.

name: Dream Realm
effect:
You burn dream-incense and lie down among the sleepers, and step sideways into a realm of your own shaping, a twilight country of still water and soft stars where minds may meet. The rite consumes 5000 coins worth of dream-incense and offerings whether or not it succeeds.
On a success, you draw yourself and up to six willing creatures who sleep near you, or who you are connected to across any distance within the same sphere of existence, into the shared dream for as long as you remain asleep within it. Within the dream, apply the following:
- All who share the dream can see and speak with one another freely, however far apart their sleeping bodies lie, and remember all that passed on waking.
- You may shape the dream's country as you wish, calling up a remembered place for the sleepers to walk, so that a memory can be revisited and examined together.
- A creature that completes an hour of unbroken sleep within the dream gains the full benefit of a night's rest and +1 additional Resolve from the deep dreaming. A creature can gain the benefit of a night's rest only once in a day, whether they take it in full through the night or swiftly through this dream. The dream shortens a rest, it never grants a second one, and it never counts as a full rest.
The dream cannot harm those within it, and it ends for any sleeper the moment their body wakes. Sleeping bodies remain in the waking world, defenseless and unaware, for the whole of the rite.

name: Shadowform
effect:
You breathe out and unmake your body into a thin ribbon of living shadow, cool and silent, that pours along walls and under doors.
On a success, you take shadowform for a short duration while you concentrate, and gain the following:
- You are immune to physical damage and cannot be grappled, restrained, or knocked prone.
- You can pass through any solid object as though it were open air, and you move up to a close distance in any direction each turn, clinging to surfaces and slipping through the narrowest seam.
- You gain +1 boon on rolls to cast twilight spells.
- When you make a physical attack or cast a spell that deals damage, you must gather substance for the act. Until the start of your next turn you have resistance to physical damage instead of immunity to it.
- You cannot carry or manipulate any object beyond a weapon you already hold.
- You carry the gloom with you. The area within melee range of you is dimmed and counts as lightly obscured, and you yourself always stand in shadow, whatever the light around you.
- Mundane light, however bright, cannot force you from this form. Only magical bright light that expels darkness forces you back into flesh and ends this spell.
You can end the spell at will, returning to your body in an open space you occupy.

name: Fate's Decree
effect:
You read a thread of destiny in the dark between the stars and speak it aloud, and fortune bends to the word. Choose one mode when you cast this spell.
Doom. Target one enemy creature of a tier equal to or lower than your Mysticism, using your roll's result vs. their Resist. On a success, for a short duration while you concentrate, once on each of their turns when they make a roll, you reduce its Success Level by one step (a weak success becomes a failure).
Blessing. Target one willing ally. On a success, for a short duration while you concentrate, once on each of their turns, they may increase the Success Level of one roll by one step (maximum critical success).

name: Phantom World
effect:
You draw the dreaming out over the waking world, and within a stretch of ground the land itself becomes a lie of your telling: false walls, false faces, false paths, all obedient to your will.
On a success, the phantom world fills the area in medium range of the target location for a medium duration while you concentrate. You choose what every creature inside perceives, reshaping the seeming of terrain, structures, and faces as you direct, and you may change it on each of your turns.
Target each creature within the area, using your roll's result vs. their Resist. On a success, that creature is affected by the phantom world. On a failure, they perceive the truth as a faint shimmer over the lie. When a creature enters the area for the first time later, roll Spirit + Mysticism vs. their Resist to determine this instead. Creatures of a tier higher than your Mysticism and creatures without a mind are never affected.
An affected creature perceives the phantom world as real and acts on it, though the substance beneath is unchanged: a phantom wall stops no one who does not believe in it, and no illusion within deals true harm.
At the start of each of your turns, choose any number of affected creatures. The false world gnaws at their minds and they take 6 psychic damage (ignore AV).
Once per turn when you reshape the phantom world, choose one affected creature. They are briefly confused as the lie closes in around them.
When a confused creature within the phantom world rolls their Confusion Check, you choose the result instead of rolling.
A creature that sees through the illusion, per its illusory property, is freed from the phantom world and all of its effects, and perceives the truth as a faint shimmer over the lie.

name: Inexorable Doom
effect:
You speak a creature's death aloud, calling every sickness, terror, and curse that clings to them home at once, and then you name the doom that follows.
Target one creature of a tier equal to or lower than your Mysticism. Count their afflictions, to a maximum of four: each condition they currently suffer among poisoned, frightened, bleeding, slowed, dazed, staggered, and stunned, and each curse spell affecting them.
Weak. Deal +4 necrotic damage, plus +6 necrotic damage for each affliction counted.
Strong. Deal +8 necrotic damage, plus +6 necrotic damage for each affliction counted.
Critical. Deal +12 necrotic damage, plus +6 necrotic damage for each affliction counted.
Also on a success, all counted conditions on the target end and all counted curse spells on them are lifted, and the doom takes hold of the target as a curse. The doom lasts for a short duration, extended by one duration step for each affliction counted. While the doom lasts, the target cannot regain HP, an Attribute die of your choice is reduced by one step, and they take 8 necrotic damage (ignore AV) at the start of each of their turns. They can roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the doom early.

name: Pall of the Grave
effect:
You call up the cold dread of the realm below, and it settles over the field like a fog the living feel in their bones. Beneath it, the grave has claim on all things.
On a success, the pall fills the area in medium range of the target location for a medium duration while you concentrate. While the pall holds, apply the following effects:
- The grave denies life within the pall. No creature inside it can regain HP.
- Any living creature that starts their turn within the pall takes 6 necrotic damage (ignore AV) as the grave's chill seeps into them.
- At the start of each of your turns, roll Spirit + Mysticism vs. Resist against all living creatures within the pall. On a success, that creature is frightened of the pall for a short duration. On a failure, that creature is immune to this fear effect for a short duration.
- While a creature is frightened of the pall, they count as afflicted for the purposes of your death spells that reward afflicted targets. If such a spell counts the number of afflictions on them, they count as suffering the maximum number instead.
- The realm below calls its own home. Undead and Spirit creatures within the pall suffer +1 bane on all rolls, and any Undead or Spirit creature slain within the pall passes on to the realm below and cannot be raised or reanimated again.

name: Ancestral Convergence
effect:
You pour out an offering and call the honored dead by name, and they come, not as servants dragged from rest, but as elders standing at the shoulders of the living.
On a success, choose up to four allies in range. An ancestral spirit attends each of them for a medium duration while you concentrate.
Once per turn, an attended ally may call on their ancestor as a special free action that works like an additional Quick Action. Even a condition that prevents Quick Actions does not prevent this call, as the spirit acts in their stead. They choose one of the following:
- Ward me. In response to taking damage, the spirit throws itself before the blow. Reduce that damage by 8.
- Steady me. In response to suffering the frightened, dazed, slowed, staggered, blinded, or confused condition, the spirit's cold hands settle their body and mind. That condition ends and they are briefly immune to it.
- Bear me. During their turn, the spirit carries them up to a short distance. This movement costs no Movement, ignores difficult terrain, passes through creatures, and is unprovoked.
The first time an attended ally would suffer a Wound, their ancestor steps between them and the blow. They do not suffer that Wound, and the spirit that shielded them departs, its duty fulfilled. This spell's effects end for that ally.

name: Wellspring of Life
effect:
You open the wellspring that runs beneath all living things and bind your companions to it, and one life flows through many bodies like water through a valley.
On a success, you and up to three willing allies in range are bonded for a medium duration. Apply the following effects:
- Once on each of their turns, a bonded creature may pour their own life into another bonded creature within short range of them as a special free action. They lose any amount of their HP (they cannot drop below 1 HP this way) and the other bonded creature regains that much HP. This is neither damage nor healing and cannot be increased, reduced, or prevented.
- When a bonded creature would be restored above their maximum HP, the excess healing flows to the most wounded bonded creature within short range of them instead of being lost.
- Life given through the bond reaches even the dying. When a dying bonded creature regains HP through this spell's effects, they stabilize, wake, and keep that HP. This is an exception to the rule that regaining HP does not end dying.

name: Genesis
effect:
Through a day of rite and patient blessing you kneel on wounded ground and call life back into it, pressing seed and clean water and green intention into soil that had forgotten how to grow. The rite consumes 5000 coins worth of blessed seed, water, and offerings whether or not it succeeds.
On a success, you restore a bounded tract of blighted, defiled, poisoned, or magically corrupted land, up to a valley or a single holding across, to natural fertility. The transformation unfolds over the hours following the rite, never in an instant: any unnatural blight, poison, or desecration on the ground drains away, including a magical corruption such as a Desecration, and the first clean water and green shoots appear by the next dawn. Over the following season, crops and wild growth take root and the land becomes able to sustain life again.
While the genesis magic slowly fades over that first season, the young land overflows with borrowed life and grants these boons within its bounds:
- The land offers itself to the living. Rolls to forage, hunt, or find water gain +1 boon and never come up entirely empty.
- Rest comes easily in the young green. A night's rest within the land removes 1 additional point of Fatigue, and a night spent there can never be reduced to a bad night.
- Healing herbs spring up wherever they are needed. Rolls to treat or heal Wounds gain +1 boon.
When the season turns, these boons fade and the land remains simply fertile and alive.
You can also cast this rite on naturally barren ground. The land answers only weakly: over the course of several hours, a single patch up to a medium distance across quickens into a lasting oasis, garden, or spring with clean water and green growth. The oasis grants none of the boons above, and the barren land beyond it does not change.
This rite mends what was despoiled and coaxes life where it can barely hold. Beyond the bounds of its oasis, it does not turn a desert into farmland, nor conjure a harvest from nothing.

name: Cataclysmic Eruption
effect:
You drive your hands into the earth and command it to rise in wrath, and whatever the land is made of answers: soil splits, stone heaves, or sand opens like a hungry mouth.
On a success, the land erupts in the area in medium range of the target location for a medium duration while you concentrate. The area is difficult terrain. The eruption takes the shape of the ground it rises from. Apply the form matching the terrain:
Verdant Ruin (soil, sod, and living earth). Lashing vines and poisoned blooms burst through the cracks.
- Creatures within the eruption are poisoned by its thick spores while they remain inside.
- When a creature starts their turn within the eruption, the heaving ground deals 8 damage (ignore AV) to them, and they must roll Strength/Agility + Athletics vs. your Resist or be restrained by the vines. A restrained creature can repeat the roll at the end of their turns to break free.
Shattered Stone (rock, ruin, and paved streets). The ground bucks in grinding slabs and rubble rains from the shearing stone.
- Creatures within the eruption are slowed by the shuddering, treacherous ground while they remain inside.
- When a creature starts their turn within the eruption, falling rubble deals 8 damage (ignore AV) to them, and they must roll Strength/Agility + Athletics vs. your Resist or be knocked prone beneath it.
Hungry Sands (sand, dust, and loose dry earth). The ground churns in slow spirals that drag everything downward.
- Creatures within the eruption are dazed by the whipping grit while they remain inside.
- When a creature starts their turn within the eruption, the grinding sand deals 8 damage (ignore AV) to them, and they must roll Strength/Agility + Athletics vs. your Resist or sink to their waist and be restrained. A restrained creature can repeat the roll at the end of their turns to pull free.
Cast over shallow water, the eruption rises from the bed beneath in the form matching that ground, and the churned water joins the difficult terrain. The open sea has no land to command and cannot erupt.

name: Primeval Form
effect:
You call the first name of the wild, and your beast shape remembers what its kind once was, swelling into a dire form of the young world.
You can only learn and cast this spell if you know the Beast Form spell.
On a success, you take on a primeval form, whether from your natural shape or from a beast form you already wear: apply the rules of the Beast Form spell, choosing from the Companion Statistics up to Tier 5. Casting this spell ends any beast form already on you. Beyond that spell's rules, your primeval form gains the following:
- The land speaks through you. You can cast nature spells while in this form. This is an exception to the rule that you cannot cast spells while shapeshifted.
- The wild does not hinder its own. You ignore difficult terrain, and your own nature spells never harm or impede you.
- Choose one primeval adaptation when you cast this spell. It marks your form with unmistakable primal magic. Once on each of your turns, you can change your adaptation for free, your form rippling into its new aspect:
  - Venom Glands. Your fangs and claws weep glowing venom. When you hit with a natural weapon on a strong or critical hit, the target is poisoned for a short duration. You can also spit venom, making your natural weapon attacks at up to short range.
  - Hide of the Old Stone. Plates of living rock and ancient moss sheathe your body. You gain +2 AV (treat this as part of the armor bonus), and when you hit with a natural weapon on a strong or critical hit, you can push the target close.
  - Thornmane. Flowering thorn-vines wind through your pelt and trail from your limbs. Your natural weapon attacks can reach targets up to a close distance away, and any creature that hits you with a melee attack takes 4 damage (ignore AV) from the thorns.
This form lasts for a medium duration, until you end it willingly, or until you fall unconscious.

name: Primal Awakening
effect:
You lay your palms to the soil and wake the sleeping land, and grass, root, hill, and stone stir to an ancient, obedient purpose.
On a success, the land wakes in the area in medium range of the target location for a medium duration while you concentrate. While the land is awake, apply the following effects:
- The ground smooths itself before your allies. They ignore difficult terrain within the area.
- The wakened land sustains your growth. You can concentrate on up to two additional nature spells that create or command plant growth within the area, without breaking your concentration on this spell or on one another. If your concentration on this spell ends, every spell the land sustains ends with it.
- Once on each of your turns, you can command the wakened land as a Quick Action, choosing one:
  - Rise. Earth and root heave upward within the area into a wall, rampart, bridge, or stair up to a close distance across. It has 10 Parry, 10 Dodge, 8 AV, and 40 HP, and crumbles when the land falls back asleep.
  - Part. The land opens the way: undergrowth parts, rubble rolls aside, or a passage up to a close distance long opens through earth or growth. The way remains open.
  - Carry. A slow wave of ground bears any number of willing creatures within the area up to a short distance in a direction of your choice, unprovoked.
  - Seize. Roots and turf grasp at one creature within the area. Roll Spirit + Mysticism vs. their Parry or Dodge (their choice). On a success, they are restrained. At the end of their turns, they can roll Strength + Athletics vs. your Resist to break free.
The land can only wake where it lives. Bare worked stone, sand, and a ship's deck lie still and are unaffected. Terrain and structures that are magically created, warded, or otherwise protected by magic cannot be altered or moved by the wakened land.

name: Magma Geyser
effect:
You crack the skin of the world and call the fire beneath it through the wound, and a tower of lava bursts upward with a sound like a god clearing their throat.
On a success, a magma geyser erupts from an unoccupied point on the ground within range for a medium duration while you concentrate. The geyser and each space in melee range of it are difficult terrain. Any creature that enters the geyser's area or starts their turn in it takes 8 fire damage (ignore AV).
Once on each of your turns, you can spend your Quick Action to direct the geyser to erupt, sending a surge of lava in a short blast (cone) in a direction you choose. Roll Spirit + Mysticism vs. the Dodge of each creature in the cone. On a success, each creature takes 12 fire damage (ignore AV) and is briefly burning (4). The geyser vents its pressure with each eruption and then builds anew, so it can only erupt once per turn.
The geyser is a natural-looking volcanic feature. It cannot be moved once placed.

name: Eye of the Storm
effect:
You breathe out and the storm breathes in, and for one suspended moment everything within a great distance of you falls still as the eye opens around you.
On a success, you become the eye of the storm for a medium duration while you concentrate. The effect extends in long range of you, moving with you. While the eye holds, apply the following effects:
- You are immune to blast, frost, and lightning damage.
- The area within the eye is calm. All wind, rain, and natural or magical weather effects within it are stilled.
- Within the eye, your tempest spells gain +1 boon and deal +2 additional damage of their type.
- At the start of each of your turns, up to three bolts of lightning crash down at points of your choice within the eye. Each bolt targets a creature in close range of its point. Roll Spirit + Mysticism vs. their Dodge. On a success, deal 10 lightning damage (ignore AV) to that creature.
- At the edge of the eye, the storm rages all the harder for its stillness at the center. Any creature that crosses from outside the eye into it, or from inside to out, takes 6 blast damage (ignore AV) from the buffeting transition winds.
